summary: Frontend-only test of 3 new features: (1) Export Full Report PDF, (2) Inline mark edit on canvas via EditMarkPopover, (3) Multi-page PDF conditional page-nav UI. All 3 features VERIFIED WORKING. Also re-verified References-tab inline label edit, PATCH /marks endpoint integration, and modal open-from-references-arrow.

ui_bugs.issue: Ref badges REF-011, REF-012, REF-013 displayed in BOQ row remarks render as orphan/disabled (red strikethrough, cursor-not-allowed, disabled attribute) even though those marks exist and are editable from the canvas and References tab. This means BOQ ref-badge click STILL does not open the modal for these marks - now visibly disabled instead of silently no-op (UX improved over iteration 3 but root cause not fully fixed). RCA: `allMarks={marks}` is passed correctly and the fallback `rowMarks.find(...) || allMarks.find(...)` is in place, so the only way isOrphan=true is if these ref_ids are not present in the marks array at the moment of render, OR if the marks have stale references. Suspect: marks prop in BOQTable may be loaded asynchronously after first render and not re-triggering on prop change, OR the regex `/^\[(REF-\d{3})/` matches and then mark lookup fails because marks list contains these but maybe with case differences. Worth a quick log of `marks.map(m=>m.ref_id)` vs `m[1]` to confirm.

Downloaded PDF filename has a double underscore: 'Civil_Arc__FullReport.pdf' instead of 'Civil_Arc_FullReport.pdf'. Likely because projectName has a trailing space ('Civil Arc ') and regex `/[^A-Za-z0-9_\-]+/g` collapses the space to '_' producing 'Civil_Arc_' then concatenated with '_FullReport.pdf'. Cosmetic but visible to users. Fix: trim projectName before sanitizing.

action_items: Investigate BOQ ref-badge orphan styling for REF-011/012/013 - marks exist but rendered disabled. Add console log of allMarks prop length in BOQRow when isOrphan=true to verify timing/prop hydration.
Trim projectName before regex sanitize in pdfReport.js line 145 to avoid 'Civil_Arc__FullReport.pdf' double-underscore filename.

(Out-of-scope but noted) Multi-page mark.page assignment and auto page-switch on Open could not be functionally validated because seed PDF '150KL 12M (1).pdf' has only 1 page. Code path verified by inspection: saveMark uses `page: currentPage` (line 365) and onOpenMark useEffect at line 131 sets `setCurrentPage(m.page)` when m.page !== currentPage. Recommend uploading a multi-page PDF to seed data for next iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: pdfReport.js: generateFullReport correctly produces 1.3MB PDF with cover + drawing page (marks burned in) + autoTable references index. Uses jsPDF + jspdf-autotable + pdfjsLib. Renders marks via canvas composite.
EditMarkPopover.js: well-structured, uses window.confirm for delete (acceptable), autoFocus on label input, Enter=save, Escape=cancel.

DrawingMeasurement.js handleCanvasClick hit-test (line 194-199): 18px radius, only fires when !mode (idle state) AND when in 'mark' mode (line 203-206) - good UX letting users always click existing marks to edit.
Page-nav toolbar correctly gated by `numPages > 1` - data-testids prev-page/next-page/page-indicator all absent for single-page PDF, present otherwise.
pdfReport.js generateFullReport: catches per-drawing fetch errors with `continue` (line 75) but does NOT surface to user - a drawing that fails to fetch will be silently omitted from PDF. Minor.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on requested features (3/3 verified). Pre-existing BOQ ref-badge orphan rendering issue persists for 3 specific marks but is visually indicated to user (improved over silent no-op).

context_for_next_testing_agent: Civil Arc project has 12 marks all on single-page PDF '150KL 12M (1).pdf'. Canvas opens at CSS rect {left: 642, top: 114, width: 3672, height: 2517}. PDF render takes ~3500ms after modal open - always wait for canvas.width > 1000 before clicking. REF-011 is at natural (504.4, 397.6). REF-001 label is now 'TEST_Refs_76563'. REF-011 label is 'TEST_Canvas_28238'. To test multi-page behaviors functionally, upload a multi-page PDF first.

multi_page_ui.note: Correct: single-page PDF -> no page nav rendered
